subject: Containers not starting fails on RBAC enabled k8s
commit_message:
Containers not starting fails on RBAC enabled k8s

When running the OOM scripts on a RBAC enabled kubernetes,
readiness probes failed to check dependencies status as they
don't have the needed access rights.

Note for the reviewer :
 The fix has been tested on a rancher installation (k8s 1.8)
 and on a kubeadm installation (k8s 1.8), but not a previous
 versions of k8s. There might be issues with the command when
 used on pre RBAC (<1.6) versions of k8s. This should be tested
 with such version if early backward compatibility should be a
 concern.

Change-Id: I7a915fc08927cd0fc0d5ea70a75f44c1380de926
Issue-ID: OOM-349
Signed-off-by: Anaël Closson <ac2550@intl.att.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Tested and this patch does not prevent the startup of OOM in my K8s 1.7 environment.  
That said, since I never had the problem outlined in the bug I can't say  with 100% certainty that this fixes it but it seems like it will.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Just curious, did this patch originate as a result of the Cloudify work that I was assisting that team on in September?  It seems very much the result of the troubleshooting we did together.

Did you try without creating a cluster role binding per namespace?  I wonder if there is a way to modify something existing so we don't have to add a binding for each application.  Perhaps something to look at in a future iteration of this.  Again, I have had K8s, 1.5. 1.6 and 1.7 (setup by rancher) and have been using OOM in each at various stages of its development and have never had this problem with readiness and RBAC..

I think you could have also created a helm template for each instead of modifying the createAll script....Anoter optimization for later perhaps.

Patch Set 2:

Reviewing over the next couple hours

Patch Set 2:

Thanks for taking a look at this. 

 > Just curious, did this patch originate as a result of the Cloudify
 > work that I was assisting that team on in September?  It seems very
 > much the result of the troubleshooting we did together.
No, I wanted to test OOM to deploy clamps dependencies to ease development and troubleshooting. I tried with rancher then minikube and as there was something breaking the install for both I gave kubeadm a shot. 
 > 
 > Did you try without creating a cluster role binding per namespace? 
Yes, and probes couldn't check the dependency status. 

 > I wonder if there is a way to modify something existing so we don't
 > have to add a binding for each application.
The documentation states that we can use permissive RBAC permissions but it isn't recommended for obvious security reasons : https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/authorization/rbac/. Unfortunately, it cannot be configured with the kubeadm install script. 

 >  Perhaps something to
 > look at in a future iteration of this.  Again, I have had K8s, 1.5.
 > 1.6 and 1.7 (setup by rancher) and have been using OOM in each at
 > various stages of its development and have never had this problem
 > with readiness and RBAC..
It seems that by default in rancher the RBAC is not enabled (at least there's a way to enable it) : http://rancher.com/docs/rancher/v1.6/en/kubernetes/rbac/
That can explain why it's not needed rancher. 

 > 
 > I think you could have also created a helm template for each
 > instead of modifying the createAll script....Anoter optimization
 > for later perhaps.
I wasn't aware of that possibility. I'll try to free some time to look at it.

Patch Set 2:

FYI, here's what I have in the probe's logs when not using the current patch :

2017-10-11 09:55:05,390 - INFO - Checking if clamp-mariadb  is ready
2017-10-11 09:55:05,394 - ERROR - Exception when calling list_namespaced_pod: (403)
Reason: Forbidden
HTTP response headers: HTTPHeaderDict({'Date': 'Wed, 11 Oct 2017 09:55:05 GMT', 'Content-Length': '95', 'Content-Type': 'text/plain', 'X-Content-Type-Options': 'nosniff'})
HTTP response body: User "system:serviceaccount:onap-clamp:default" cannot list pods in the namespace "onap-clamp".

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Excellent!  Thanks for fixing this.  I will setup a rancher K8s env that uses non-default RBAC.

FYI: The guys from Cloudify experienced the same log as you:

HTTP response headers: HTTPHeaderDict({'Date': 'Mon, 18 Sep 2017 14:05:27 GMT', 'Content-Length': '91', 'Content-Type': 'text/plain', 'X-Content-Type-Options': 'nosniff'})
HTTP response body: User "system:serviceaccount:onap-mso:default" cannot list pods in the namespace "onap-mso".
2017-09-18 14:05:32,397 - INFO - Checking if mariadb  is ready
2017-09-18 14:05:32,403 - ERROR - Exception when calling list_namespaced_pod: (403)
Reason: Forbidden

I'll follow up with them and let them know this issue has been resolved.
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda
